title: Google Drive access denied error
symptom: When attempting to create Google Drive credentials, the user encounters 'Error 403: access denied'. This prevents successful authentication and access to Google Drive.
not_this_if: Google Drive account is already connected successfully
root_cause: The error occurs when the Google app is not properly configured for OAuth2 access. This can happen if the app is in production mode without the necessary permissions or if the required scopes are not set correctly.
fix_steps: Ensure that your Google app is set to test mode.
Add the necessary OAuth2 scopes required for Google Drive access.
Verify that the redirect URL is correctly configured in the Google Cloud Console.
agent_instruction: Set your Google app to test mode and ensure all required OAuth2 scopes are added.
